title: 7 Best Drip Alternatives in 2025 (Free & Paid)
meta_description: Compare the top alternatives to Drip email marketing. Find the best tool for e-commerce, creators, and small businesses with our detailed comparison.
h1: Best Drip Alternatives: Compare Email Marketing Tools
intro: While Drip excels at e-commerce email marketing with deep store integration, it's not the right fit for every business—especially those looking for free options or different feature sets. Whether you need affordability, advanced automation, or specialized tools for creators and newsletters, several strong alternatives offer compelling reasons to make the switch.
why_switch: Find a more affordable solution with flexible pricing or generous free tiers
Access specialized features tailored to your business type (creators, SMBs, newsletters)
Get better customer support and higher user satisfaction ratings

alternatives.name: Klaviyo
alternatives.tagline: The platform for intelligent marketing automation
alternatives.best_for: e-commerce brands on Shopify or WooCommerce
alternatives.key_difference: Klaviyo rivals Drip's e-commerce capabilities with deeper Shopify/WooCommerce integration and predictive analytics that optimize customer targeting. If you're already on these platforms and want similar functionality, Klaviyo's industry-leading features make it the closest competitor.

alternatives.name: ActiveCampaign
alternatives.best_for: businesses needing advanced automation and CRM
alternatives.key_difference: ActiveCampaign combines email marketing with a full CRM and lead scoring capabilities, offering more depth than Drip for businesses managing complex sales funnels. Starting at just $15/month, it's also significantly cheaper than Drip's $39 entry point while delivering comparable automation power.

alternatives.name: MailerLite
alternatives.tagline: Simple email marketing for small businesses
alternatives.best_for: small businesses and freelancers on a budget
alternatives.key_difference: MailerLite offers a free tier for up to 1,000 subscribers and starts paid plans at just $9/month—making it ideal if budget is a concern. It includes drag-and-drop editing, automation, and e-commerce integrations comparable to Drip at a fraction of the cost.

alternatives.name: ConvertKit
alternatives.key_difference: ConvertKit is purpose-built for creators and course sellers with native landing pages, subscriber tagging, and commerce features specifically designed for digital products. If you're monetizing content or selling courses rather than physical goods, ConvertKit's creator-focused approach outshines Drip's e-commerce focus.

alternatives.name: Beehiiv
alternatives.tagline: The newsletter platform built for growth
alternatives.key_difference: Beehiiv specializes in newsletter monetization with built-in ad networks and referral programs, plus exceptional user satisfaction (4.4 Trustpilot vs Drip's 3.5). For publishers looking to generate revenue from newsletters, Beehiiv's growth-focused tools and higher ratings make it superior.

alternatives.name: Mailchimp
alternatives.key_difference: Mailchimp's free tier and $13 entry price make it unbeatable for budget-conscious teams just starting with email marketing. While it lacks Drip's advanced e-commerce tracking, it covers the basics well and includes landing pages and A/B testing.

alternatives.name: Brevo
alternatives.key_difference: Brevo stands out with an enormous free tier (100,000 contacts) and multi-channel capabilities including SMS, WhatsApp, and live chat alongside email. For businesses needing omnichannel marketing beyond email, Brevo offers significantly more value than Drip's email-focused approach.

faq.q: What is the best free alternative to Drip?
faq.a: Brevo offers the most generous free tier with support for 100,000 contacts, making it ideal if you're bootstrapping. Alternatively, MailerLite and Mailchimp both offer free plans with up to 1,000 subscribers and include essential automation features comparable to Drip's paid functionality.

faq.q: What is the best Drip alternative for small business?
faq.a: MailerLite is the best overall choice for small businesses, combining affordability (plans start at $9/month), a free tier, e-commerce integrations, and ease of use. If your small business needs CRM features alongside email, ActiveCampaign at $15/month offers superior automation and lead management.

faq.q: Is there a cheaper alternative to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, several alternatives undercut Drip's $39 starting price: ActiveCampaign ($15), GetResponse ($19), MailerLite ($9), and others offer free or lower-cost entry points. However, if you need comparable e-commerce features, Klaviyo at $45/month is worth the investment as Drip's closest true competitor.
